flavor_text: It drives enemies out of its nest by sucking in enough air to fill its long, narrow lungs, then releasing the air in an intense blast.

name: kingambit
flavor_text: Though it commands a massive army in battle, it’s not skilled at devising complex strategies. It just uses brute strength to keep pushing.

name: great-tusk
flavor_text: This creature resembles a mysterious Pokémon that, according to a paranormal magazine, has lived since ancient times.

name: scream-tail
flavor_text: It resembles a mysterious Pokémon described in a paranormal magazine as a Jigglypuff from one billion years ago.

name: brute-bonnet
flavor_text: It bears a slight resemblance to a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a cross between a dinosaur and a mushroom.

name: flutter-mane
flavor_text: It has similar features to a ghostly pterosaur that was covered in a paranormal magazine, but the two have little else in common.

name: slither-wing
flavor_text: This Pokémon somewhat resembles an ancient form of Volcarona that was introduced in a dubious magazine.

name: sandy-shocks
flavor_text: It slightly resembles a Magneton that lived for 10,000 years and was featured in an article in a paranormal magazine.

name: iron-treads
flavor_text: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.

name: iron-bundle
flavor_text: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

name: iron-hands
flavor_text: This Pokémon shares many similarities with Iron Hands, an object mentioned in a certain expedition journal.

name: iron-jugulis
flavor_text: It’s possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

name: iron-moth
flavor_text: No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with an object described in an old book.

name: iron-thorns
flavor_text: Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

name: frigibax
flavor_text: This Pokémon lives in forests and craggy areas. Using the power of its dorsal fin, it cools the inside of its nest like a refrigerator.

name: arctibax
flavor_text: It attacks with the blade of its frozen dorsal fin by doing a front flip in the air. Arctibax’s strong back and legs allow it to pull off this technique.

name: baxcalibur
flavor_text: It launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.

name: gimmighoul
flavor_text: It lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it’s actually a Pokémon.

name: gholdengo
flavor_text: It has a sturdy body made up of stacked coins. Gholdengo overwhelms its enemies by firing coin after coin at them in quick succession.

name: wo-chien
flavor_text: It drains the life-force from vegetation, causing nearby forests to instantly wither and fields to turn barren.

name: chien-pao
flavor_text: The hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.

name: ting-lu
flavor_text: It slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.

name: chi-yu
flavor_text: The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.

name: roaring-moon
flavor_text: According to an article in a dubious magazine, this Pokémon has some connection to a phenomenon that occurs in a certain region.

name: iron-valiant
flavor_text: It’s possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

name: koraidon
flavor_text: This Pokémon resembles Cyclizar, but it is far burlier and more ferocious. Nothing is known about its ecology or other features.

name: miraidon
flavor_text: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

name: walking-wake
flavor_text: It resembles an illustration published in a paranormal magazine, said to be a depiction of a super-ancient Suicune.

name: iron-leaves
flavor_text: According to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.

name: dipplin
flavor_text: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

name: poltchageist
flavor_text: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

name: sinistcha
flavor_text: It prefers cool, dark places, such as the back of a shelf or the space beneath a home's floorboards. It wanders in search of prey after sunset.

name: okidogi
flavor_text: Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

name: munkidori
flavor_text: Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

name: fezandipiti
flavor_text: Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.

name: ogerpon
flavor_text: This mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

name: archaludon
flavor_text: It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It’s so durable that being caught in a cave-in won’t faze it.

name: hydrapple
flavor_text: These capricious syrpents have banded together. On the rare occasion that their moods align, their true power is unleashed.

name: gouging-fire
flavor_text: It resembles an eerie Pokémon once shown in a paranormal magazine. That Pokémon was said to be an Entei regenerated from a fossil.

name: raging-bolt
flavor_text: It bears resemblance to a Pokémon that became a hot topic for a short while after a paranormal magazine touted it as Raikou's ancestor.

name: iron-boulder
flavor_text: It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

name: iron-crown
flavor_text: There was supposedly an incident in which it launched shining blades to cut everything around it to pieces. Little else is known about it.

name: terapagos
flavor_text: It’s thought that this Pokémon lived in ancient Paldea until it got caught in seismic shifts and went extinct.

name: pecharunt
flavor_text: Its peach-shaped shell serves as storage for a potent poison. It makes poisonous mochi and serves them to people and Pokémon.
